author: Santi López Puiggené
source_document: Computational parameter specification — v1.4.0. Parameter values define the abstract simulation space. They are not derived from or validated against HFSS, CST, or any other professional electromagnetic simulation tool.

IV_network_performance_metrics.individual_q_factor_note: Q < 1 places the model in the overdamped regime. Coherence and merit metrics remain valid as comparative structural indicators across morphologies.

VI_experimental_sweep_parameters.noise_level_note: Symmetric Gaussian perturbation (sigma) applied identically to every morphology at every sweep step. Single source of truth — read by node_coupling.py and parametric_sweep.py.
VI_experimental_sweep_parameters.connection_radius_note: Union-find adjacency radius for topology validation. The botanical morphology reaches the widest extent (max |coordinate| around 4.05 units); the radius is the local adjacency scale, not the full bounding box.
VI_experimental_sweep_parameters.variance_collapse_fraction_note: Relative arm of the variance guard: a morphology whose per-seed std falls below this fraction of the cross-morphology median std is flagged as seed-frozen and reported as n/a in inference_analysis.py. Evaluated by stats_utils.near_zero_variance alongside the absolute variance_floor.
VI_experimental_sweep_parameters.variance_floor_note: Absolute arm of the variance guard: any standard deviation below this value is treated as near-zero. Shared by cohens_d and the multi-seed seed-frozen check through the single authority stats_utils.near_zero_variance, so the pooled-effect-size guard and the inference guard can never disagree.
VI_experimental_sweep_parameters.multi_seed_list_note: 30 consecutive seeds for multi-seed inference analysis. Per-morphology means computed per seed; Welch t-test + Holm-Bonferroni + bootstrap CI applied to the N=30 i.i.d. per-seed means in inference_analysis.py.
VI_experimental_sweep_parameters.curve_separation_threshold_note: Minimum signed curve-separation magnitude required for finding_holds=True in the parametric robustness sweep. A point-by-seed cell holds only when the botanical merit mean sits BELOW both the random and the Voronoi controls by at least this amount (curve_sep <= -threshold against each), so the sweep tests the directional v1.3.0 claim rather than mere magnitude. Single source of truth for parametric_sweep.py.

VII_array_factor_parameters.k0_base_note: Heuristic spatial scaling coefficient. Not the electromagnetic wave number k0 = 2*pi*f/c. This parameter operates in abstract simulation space and has no physical-frequency interpretation.
VII_array_factor_parameters.phase_rule_note: Node phase is assigned from each node's spatial position relative to the point-set centroid, making the array factor invariant to the arbitrary order in which a generator emits its nodes. 'sector' (primary) quantises the centroid-relative angle into four quadrants mapped to [0, pi/2, pi, 3pi/2]; 'continuous' (used only by the phase-robustness check in phase_robustness.py) uses the centroid-relative angle directly. This replaces the v1.3.0 index-based phase base_phases[i % 4], which bound the array factor to generator node ordering and produced the now-retracted botanical separation. Single source of truth for node_coupling.py.

VIII_pipeline.morphologies_note: Ten morphologies in v1.4.0. fractal, fibonacci and hexagonal are geometrically deterministic (seed-frozen, cosmetic jitter only); botanical, random, voronoi, dla, clusters, concentric and reticulate carry genuine between-seed geometric variance. Single source of truth — read by run.py, multi_seed_analysis.py, inference_analysis.py, topology_analysis.py, plot_sensitivity.py and the integrity tests.

IX_topology_parameters.k_primary_note: Primary neighbour count for the union k-NN graph in graph_topology.py. Spectral and clustering metrics reported in the main analysis use this k.
IX_topology_parameters.k_robustness_note: Additional neighbour counts swept by topology_analysis.py to check that any morphology-to-merit correlation is not an artefact of a single graph resolution.
IX_topology_parameters.correlation_threshold_note: Critical absolute Pearson r for N=10 morphologies (df=8, two-tailed alpha=0.05). A correlation between a topology metric and merit at the morphology level is flagged as passing only when |r| exceeds this value. Single source of truth for topology_analysis.py.
IX_topology_parameters.metrics_note: H1 tests eigenratio R = lambda_max / lambda_2 against scaled merit; H2 tests algebraic connectivity lambda_2 against scaled merit; H3 tests the average clustering coefficient against scaled merit. All three are reported honestly with no pre-registration.
